nicegeo.diagnostics.debounceMs.description: Debounce delay in milliseconds for on-type diagnostics.

nicegeo.execution.mode.description: How nicegeo commands are launched. Use dune to run in the project, or installedBinary to run the nicegeo binaries from PATH.

nicegeo.proofState.followCursor.description: While proof mode is on and the NiceGeo Proof State view is visible, refresh proof state as the cursor moves (tactic prefix before the cursor).

nicegeo.proofState.followCursorDebounceM.description: Debounce for follow-cursor proof state updates (milliseconds).

walkthroughs.title: NiceGeo: Quick start
walkthroughs.description: Quick tour of NiceGeo features and setup.

steps.title: What is NiceGeo IDE?
steps.description:
**NiceGeo IDE** adds language intelligence for **`.ncg`** in VS Code.

It includes diagnostics, completion, hover, go-to-definition, Proof Mode with the Proof State sidebar, and optional visual tooling.

Read the overview and continue.

media.altText: NiceGeo quick start overview with editor and proof state

steps.title: Open NiceGeo settings
steps.description:
Open filtered Settings for **NiceGeo** and review all key categories: execution, diagnostics, and proof-state behavior.

Use the visual overview on the right, then open settings to apply your defaults.

[Open NiceGeo settings](command:nicegeo.walkthrough.openSettings)

steps.title: Diagnostics feature
steps.description:
Open the guided feature file at the diagnostics section. Follow the comment there for what to do and what to expect.

[See in action](command:nicegeo.walkthrough.openFeatureDiagnostics)

steps.title: Autocompletion feature
steps.description:
Open the guided feature file at the autocompletion section. Follow the in-file comment for action and expected behavior.

[See in action](command:nicegeo.walkthrough.openFeatureCompletion)

steps.title: Hover feature
steps.description:
Open the guided feature file at the hover section. The comment tells where to place the cursor and what hover should show.

[See in action](command:nicegeo.walkthrough.openFeatureHover)

steps.title: Go-to-definition feature
steps.description:
Open the guided feature file at the go-to-definition section. Follow the comment and press `F12` on the target symbol.

[See in action](command:nicegeo.walkthrough.openFeatureGoto)

steps.title: Proof state feature
steps.description:
Open the guided feature file at the proof-state section. Then enter Proof Mode and observe updates while moving through tactics.

[See in action](command:nicegeo.walkthrough.openFeatureProofState)

steps.title: Manual commands (granular control)
steps.description:
Most behavior is automated from settings. If you need fine control, use categorized commands from the palette.

[Open Command Palette](command:workbench.action.showCommands)

media.altText: Categorized NiceGeo commands for manual control

steps.title: Explore more and contribute
steps.description:
Continue with deeper hands-on learning and join project development.

- [Open the Getting Started: Proof Mode tutorial](command:nicegeo.walkthrough.openProofModeWalkthrough)
- [Contribute to the project on GitHub](https://github.com/nicegeo/nicegeo)

When done, [mark this step complete](command:nicegeo.walkthrough.ackQuickstartMore).

walkthroughs.title: NiceGeo: Proof Mode tutorial
walkthroughs.description: Learn Proof Mode with live goal tracking.

steps.title: Proof Mode in brief
steps.description:
In **`Proof.` … `Qed.`** scripts, the server can report proof state **up to the cursor**. Moving the caret before vs after the end of a tactic line changes whether that tactic is applied in the snapshot; after **`Qed.`**, the whole script counts as done.

The **Proof State** view shows goals, hypotheses, and related detail as you move.

Read the summary on the right, then continue.

media.altText: Proof mode cursor behavior and proof state overview

steps.title: Open the tutorial file
steps.description:
Open the bundled tutorial: it **leads with several `Proof.` … `Qed.` proofs**, then hover/go-to-definition practice, then a small term-style contrast at the end.

It opens **beside** this walkthrough. The path is inside the extension install (read-only); use **File → Save As…** for a workspace copy.

[Open walkthrough tutorial](command:nicegeo.walkthrough.openProofTutorial)

steps.title: Enter Proof Mode
steps.description:
Focus the **tutorial** tab, then enable proof tracking so the sidebar can query the server.

[Enter Proof Mode](command:nicegeo.proofMode.enter)

steps.title: Show Proof State
steps.description:
Open **NiceGeo** in the activity bar, then **Proof State**. You can also [show the NiceGeo side bar](command:workbench.view.extension.nicegeoSidebar).

Click inside the **first** `Proof.` … `Qed.` block in the tutorial and move line-by-line — watch goals update.

media.altText: Open NiceGeo sidebar and inspect proof state while moving cursor

steps.title: Explore the tutorial
steps.description:
Work through the file **top to bottom**: several tactic scripts, then **hover** / **F12** on `walkthrough_dup`, `TrueI`, and locals, then notice the final **term-only** theorem (no `Proof.`) as a contrast.

[Mark this step complete](command:nicegeo.walkthrough.ackProofPlay)
